metadata.title: Evaluate an MCP Server Before Connecting It with Claude
metadata.note: CLI video. Exclusions: MCP protocol implementation details, OWASP MCP Top 10 full walkthrough, custom server development.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'NikBearBrownOpen' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

beats.role_note: the problem, context, stakes -- BEFORE the CLI opens
beats.narration_text: Server A reads tickets. Server B reads and writes them. The task is a status report -- read-only. Connecting Server B grants write access you did not ask for. The review checklist shows which server to connect and why before the agent runs.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- title card: CONNECTING AN MCP SERVER IS AN APPROVAL GATE. The moment of connection determines every downstream risk.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Server A reads tickets. Server B reads and writes them. The task is a status report -- read-only. Connecting Server B grants write access you did not ask for. T

beats.narration_text: Ask Claude to evaluate two servers against the status-report task -- what each can do, blast radius, required approval gate, and a connect-or-not recommendation.

props.command: claude "Evaluate two MCP servers for a 'weekly status report from open tickets' task. Server A: read-only ticket resources. Server B: ticket resources plus tools to update status and post comments. For each: what can the agent do, blast radius on error, one required approval gate, recommend connect or do-not-connect."

beats.narration_text: The MCP evaluator formats each server as a scored row: server name, what the agent can do, blast radius, approval gate required, and final recommendation.

props.code:
SERVERS = [
 {"name":"Server A","can_do":"read tickets",
  "blast":"low","gate":"none required",
  "rec":"CONNECT"},
 {"name":"Server B","can_do":"read+write tickets,post comments",
  "blast":"high","gate":"human approval before write",
  "rec":"CONDITIONAL"},
]
hdr = "SERVER    CAN-DO              BLAST  GATE            REC"
print(hdr); print("-"*len(hdr))
for s in SERVERS:
    print(f"{s['name']:<10}{s['can_do']:<20}{s['blast']:<7}"
          f"{s['gate']:<16}{s['rec']}")

beats.narration_text: Two-server comparison prints. Server A gets CONNECT. Server B gets CONDITIONAL -- write access requires a human gate before any ticket modification.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- screen-recording: terminal showing two-row evaluation table, Server A marked CONNECT, Server B marked CONDITIONAL with gate requirement noted

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Two-server comparison prints. Server A gets CONNECT. Server B gets CONDITIONAL -- write access requires a human gate before any ticket modification.

beats.narration_text: Add Server C with external email capability. What is the highest concern for a server that can send email from within the agent context?

props.command: claude "Add Server C: reads tickets plus sends external email. What is the highest concern for this server in an agentic context?"

beats.narration_text: Server C flagged: highest concern is prompt injection. External email means an adversarial ticket could instruct the agent to send unauthorized messages.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- screen-recording: terminal showing three-row table with Server C flagged DO-NOT-CONNECT with highest concern: PROMPT INJECTION via external email capability

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Server C flagged: highest concern is prompt injection. External email means an adversarial ticket could instruct the agent to send unauthorized messages.

beats.narration_text: Connecting an MCP server is an approval gate. The capabilities you grant at connection are the capabilities the agent can misuse. Blast radius and reversibility are the two columns that determine whether a gate is required.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- recap card: CONNECTION = CAPABILITY GRANT. Blast radius and reversibility determine the gate tier.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Connecting an MCP server is an approval gate. The capabilities you grant at connection are the capabilities the agent can misuse. Blast radius and reversibility

beats.narration_text: Before connecting any MCP server, score it on blast radius and reversibility. If either is high, require a human approval gate before any write or send action.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- next-steps card: Score blast radius and reversibility before connecting. High on either = human gate required.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Before connecting any MCP server, score it on blast radius and reversibility. If either is high, require a human approval gate before any write or send action.

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Nik Bear Brown, nikbearbrown dot com.
